content.full_text:
May 15, 1991

Jerry D. Wood
Crabtree, Karlen & Arzoo
6355 Riverside Avenue
Riverside, CA 92506-3145

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-90-388a (Follow-up)

Dear Mr. Wood:

This is to advise you that, upon further consideration by the Commission, the advice we gave to you in the Wood Advice Letter, No. I-90-388, dated June 25, 1990, has been revised.

In your letter to the Commission, you sought confirmation of telephone advice. Specifically, and in pertinent part, you sought advice as to whether you must request employer and occupational information from a contributor from whom you have received a contribution of $25 or more. We informed you that you must request such information from the contributor; however, it was not necessary for you to return the contribution if the contributor did not provide the requested information.

The Commission considered the request for reconsideration that was filed by Allan Tebbetts, chair of the California Political Attorneys Association, on your behalf, and the oral and written comments of interested persons. It also heard testimony from the Acting Chief of Enforcement. Based on the evidence in the administrative record before it, the Commission determined that a candidate, elected officer, or committee treasurer must request the name of the employer and occupation of the contributor only when contributions from that contributor aggregate $100 or more.

Please contact me at (916) 322-5901 if you have any further questions regarding this advice.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

[Signature]
By: Deanne Stone
Counsel, Legal Division
